pr165445.title: Crash with C++20 modules and header units in clang 21
pr165445.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes while precompiling a C++20 module that consumes a header unit. The public fix explains that enum-constant visibility lookup in ASTWriter assumes a named owning module, but header units make getTopLevelOwningNamedModule return null, so module serialization crashes in GenerateNameLookupTable.

pr54556.title: GNU ObjC frontend crash in clang 14 building Objective-C++ code
pr54556.bug_report_summary: Clang 14 regressed on Objective-C++ with the GNU runtime: compiling an instance-method call that passes a non-trivial C++ value such as std::string crashes during IR generation, with the stack rooted at clang::CodeGen::CreateGNUObjCRuntime and EmitObjCMessageExpr.

pr176682.title: Miscompilation at -O3 with profiled data using PGO
pr176682.bug_report_summary: Reduced LLVM miscompilation: plain -O3 on x86_64 prints 1, but -O3 with PGO using llvm-profdata prints 0. The local bisect and later analysis point to SelectionDAG legalization behavior around widened build_vector lanes.

pr187875.title: Miscompilation at -Os
pr187875.bug_report_summary: Reduced LLVM miscompilation: the program prints 55 without size optimization, but prints 53 at -Os. The existing local analysis shows the trigger commit was in loop vectorization and the visible semantic failure flowed through extract.last.active and SelectionDAG lowering.

pr191581.title: Miscompilation at -Os with profiled data using PGO
pr191581.bug_report_summary: Reduced LLVM miscompilation: plain -Os prints 9, but -Os with PGO using llvm-profdata prints 1. The local bisect ended in a LICM-related change, so loop transforms, hoisting, aliasing, and profile-sensitive optimizations are relevant risk areas.

pr172195.title: Clang crashes on valid code when compiling with profiled data using PGO
pr172195.bug_report_summary: LLVM frontend crash on valid C code with PGO: -O3 with profile data compiles, but -Os with -fprofile-instr-use crashes in loop-vectorize, with the stack trace pointing at LoopVectorizationCostModel::expectedCost on func_21. The known first bad commit from the email bisect is a VPlan change.

pr170421.title: Crash with PCH and -ftime-trace
pr170421.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes when compiling a source file with -ftime-trace while including a precompiled header. The issue report provides both the documented first bad commit 97fdc23 and the later fix commit 4e859c5a95ec; the LM-bisect profile normalizes that into the searchable interval regression^..fix^ so the first bad commit remains the documented regression inside the window.

pr193932.title: Crash in clang codegen with -gtemplate-alias
pr193932.good_ref: llvmorg-20.1.0 (issue says Clang 20.0.0 works; 20.1.0 used as local good anchor)
pr193932.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes in debug info code generation for a template-alias reproducer compiled with -O2 -gtemplate-alias. The issue text states the crash has been present since commit 366f48890d64 and that Clang 20.0.0 works, so the current LM-bisect setup uses a local Clang 20 tag as the good anchor and the documented regression commit itself as the bad anchor.

pr195788.title: Crash in clangd on pseudo-destructor selection
pr195788.bug_report_summary: clangd crashes during an LSP codeAction reproducer involving pseudo-destructor selection. The usable benchmark interval is the validated older good anchor llvmorg-15.0.7 to the validated bad parent of PR #195939.

pr196450.title: Crash in constexpr designated initialization
pr196450.bug_report_summary: Clang hits a frontend assertion while constexpr-evaluating a designated initializer with placement new under -std=c++2c. The current profile now uses the validated forward interval from llvmorg-19.1.7 to llvmorg-22.1.0 for real bisect runs.

pr196244.title: Crash in clangd token collection
pr196244.bug_report_summary: clangd crashes during an LSP documentSymbol reproducer on a deeply nested lambda testcase. The profile intentionally uses a long benchmark interval from llvmorg-8.0.1, which is good, to llvmorg-22.1.0, which is bad, even though the narrow validated release boundary is llvmorg-8.0.1 to llvmorg-9.0.1.

pr65982.title: Crash in C++20 virtual base name lookup
pr65982.bug_report_summary: Clang hits a frontend assertion while compiling a C++20 reproducer with virtual bases and pack-expanded using-declarations. The current profile now uses the validated forward interval from llvmorg-17.0.6 to the reporter crash build for real bisect runs.

pr190445.title: APInt ZeroExtend assertion while evaluating alloc_size
pr190445.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes with an APInt ZeroExtend assertion while compiling a reduced C++ reproducer with debug info and assembly output. The stack points through APInt::zext into CallExpr::evaluateBytesReturnedByAllocSizeCall and Sema::ActOnCastExpr, so alloc_size evaluation, APInt width handling, cast expressions, and Sema are the primary risk areas.

pr202343.title: Crash in ScalarEvolution ExitLimit at -O3
pr202343.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes at -O3 on a reduced C testcase with an assertion in ScalarEvolution::ExitLimit: no point in having a non-constant max backedge taken count. The crash occurs while the optimizer runs loop-rotate and loop-deletion, so ScalarEvolution, loop analysis, and loop transforms are the primary risk areas.

pr192829.title: clang-tidy crash in readability-container-size-empty
pr192829.bug_report_summary: clang-tidy crashes when running readability-container-size-empty on code that explicitly calls std::string's pseudo-destructor. The assertion says NamedDecl::getName requires a simple identifier, and the stack is inside AST matchers and clang-tidy's readability check.

pr120802.title: Clang crash on consteval lambda decltype instantiation
pr120802.bug_report_summary: Clang crashes compiling a C++20 consteval template that returns a lambda with decltype over another lambda call. The stack reaches ASTContext::getTypeInfo and Sema return-type deduction during template instantiation, so Sema, AST layout/type information, and lambda instantiation are the main risk areas.

skip-pr199918-riscv.title: RISCV CodeGen build failure after Zvzip interleave shuffle change
skip-pr199918-riscv.good_ref: validated wide good, 1000 first-parent commits before bad
skip-pr199918-riscv.bug_report_summary: LLVM RISCV CodeGen fails to build after PR #199512, with RISCVISelLowering.cpp passing too many arguments to isLegalVTForZvzipOperand. The revert PR #199899 fixes the source build failure. This benchmark studies whether build-failure evidence from a real source-level compile error helps LM-Bisect localize the bad commit.
